Validate photo IDs in response before accepting fetch result

The Cloudflare challenge page on GH Actions contains lomography.com
references, so generic content validation passes. Instead, validate at
the parsing level: only accept a fetch if we actually find photo IDs in
the response. Both curl and cloudscraper are now tried on each attempt,
with retries and exponential backoff.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Lomography workflow failing silently on Cloudflare blocks

- Add content validation (is_valid_lomography_response) to detect when
  curl/cloudscraper returns a Cloudflare challenge page instead of real
  Lomography content. Previously the >1000 byte check passed for
  challenge pages, skipping the cloudscraper fallback entirely.
- When 0 photos are found from the profile page, exit with error code 1
  instead of silently reporting no new photos. This makes the workflow
  fail visibly so the issue is noticed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add visual-proposer agent and make specialist rules research-first (#14)

* feat: add whytcard-visual-proposer agent and visual proposal sprint step in /wi-whytcard workflow

Adds a new shipped specialist `whytcard-visual-proposer` that generates
8–12 standalone HTML mockups with varied layouts, color palettes,
typography, text/copy, and component styles. The /wi-whytcard workflow
now includes an explicit visual proposal sprint step (3b) that delegates
to this agent when the project has a UI, presents proposals to the user,
and records the chosen direction before implementation begins.

Co-authored-by: Whyt-AI <263242501+Whyt-AI@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Whyt-AI/WhytCard-Intelligence/sessions/ca421b7a-6037-49fe-bdd0-5ae4f0e68a46

* feat: make specialist rules research-first instead of hardcoded static

All specialist-facing rule files (.mdc) and agent definitions now include
a research-first preamble that instructs agents to search for current
best practices, latest standards (WCAG, OWASP, framework docs), and
modern techniques before applying the baseline rules.

The existing rules are preserved as a safe fallback baseline, but agents
are now explicitly told to research and adapt when current docs recommend
better approaches. This makes the system dynamic and resilient to
framework updates, standard revisions, and evolving best practices.

commit.message:
fix: treat Geant4 kInfinity as no-intersection in TraceRay (#255)

Geant4's kInfinity (CLHEP) is 9.9999e+99, a large but finite double.
std::isinf() returns false for it, so rays where DistanceToIn returns
kInfinity (no intersection) were being treated as valid hits.

This caused SurfaceNormal to be evaluated at kInfinity * direction,
a nonsensical point far outside the solid, producing garbage normals
and false surface-normal mismatches.  For G4TwistedTubs (dphi=120°),
this accounted for all 1381 reported surface-normal failures.

Fix:
- DistanceString: also print "kInfinity" for values >= kInfinity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct G4ScaledSolid exit normals in DistanceToOut fixture (#253)

* fix: correct G4ScaledSolid exit normals in DistanceToOut fixture

G4ScaledSolid::DistanceToOut (Geant4 bug, present through at least 11.4.1)
calls fScale->TransformNormal() on the exit normal returned by the unscaled
base solid.  TransformNormal maps normals from global→local frame, but the
base-solid normal (solNorm) is already in the local (unscaled) frame.  The
correct transform is InverseTransformNormal() (local→global), which applies
the inverse-transpose of the scale matrix (divide each component by its
scale factor).  G4ScaledSolid::SurfaceNormal() uses InverseTransformNormal()
correctly, explaining why SurfaceNormal yields 0 mismatches while
DistanceToOut exit normals show 2039/2048 failures for the non-uniformly
scaled sphere fixture (semi-axes 15×20×5 mm).

Fix in G4OCCT: introduce G4ScaledSolidFixed, a minimal subclass that
overrides DistanceToOut to recompute the exit normal via SurfaceNormal()
at the exit point.  SurfaceNormal() is correctly implemented in Geant4,
so this gives the geometrically correct outward normal without any
modification to the Geant4 source tree.  The fixture builder now constructs
G4ScaledSolidFixed instead of G4ScaledSolid for native-solid comparisons.

Remove the broad G4ScaledSolid xfail in fixture_validation.cc (reason was
stale: 'not implemented yet').  Add a comment explaining the Geant4 bug and
the workaround.  Document the bug and fix strategy in provenance.yaml.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* Update src/tests/fixtures/geometry/wrapper-decorator/G4ScaledSolid/scaled-sphere-nonuniform-v1/provenance.yaml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Property index redesign (#39)

* Add preload option to Properties.list_properties/2

Move association preloading into the context to maintain proper
boundary separation and allow LiveViews to specify required preloads
without directly accessing Repo.

* Redesign property index LiveView with enhanced UI

Complete overhaul of the property listing page with:
- Responsive desktop table layout with status badges
- Mobile card layout for better mobile experience
- Property occupancy status badges (occupied/upcoming/vacant)
- Icon-only action buttons (view, edit, delete)
- Enhanced empty state design
- Improved error handling in delete action
- Add user_id verification in PubSub message handling
- Stream-based rendering for performance

* Update property LiveView tests for new icon-only buttons

Fix test selectors to use title attributes instead of text content
since action buttons are now icon-only without text labels.

* Make property items clickable to navigate to show page

- Add cursor-pointer class to table rows and mobile cards
- Wrap property content cells in links that navigate to property show page
- Remove redundant View buttons from desktop and mobile layouts
- Keep Edit and Delete actions functional and separate from navigation

* Refactor property index to use row-level click handling

Replace nested <.link> components in table cells with phx-click
on the entire row for navigation. Add phx-stop to action buttons
to prevent click propagation. Change action button sizes to btn-md.

* Update lib/vivvo/properties.ex

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update lib/vivvo_web/live/property_live/index.ex

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Subscribe to contract broadcasts in property index LiveView

Property status depends on associated contracts, but the LiveView only
subscribed to property change broadcasts. This meant creating/updating/
archiving a contract wouldn't trigger property status updates, causing
stale badges until manual refresh.

Now also subscribes to contract broadcasts and refreshes properties and
property_statuses when contract events occur.
